title: alpinia werneri
intro: Alpinia werneri, also known as the Lesser Galangal, is a type of perennial plant in the ginger family (Zingiberaceae) native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant's rhizome has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Alpinia werneri include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Alpinia werneri can be prepared as a tea, powder, or oil, and is also used in traditional cooking and herbal remedies.
